title: Autonomous micro-drone achieves first air-to-air insect kill on the way 'towards completely eradicating mosquitoes' 40-gram unit uses car parking sensors, can eliminate insects at up to 26 feet
summary: A micro-drone has successfully eliminated a mosquito in mid-air, marking a crucial step towards eradicating the species.
insight: Potential for disease prevention through autonomous, high-accuracy insect control.

title: ASML's planned Low-NA EUV machine price hikes reportedly frustrate TSMC lithography machine maker comes knocking to make bank on TSMC's profitable fabs, potentially costing the Taiwanese chipmaker billions
summary: ASML's planned price hike for Low-NA EUV tools may impact TSMC's expansion plans, as the foundry invested heavily in existing lithography systems. ASML's increased productivity gives it the option to raise prices in the future. This move could cost TSMC billions.
insight: TSMC's expansion plans threatened by ASML's price hike option.

title: TSMC confirms significant yield and performance improvements in A14 update strong interest from AI/HPC and smartphone customers
summary: TSMC's A14 process technology has shown significant yield and performance improvements, with strong interest from AI/HPC and smartphone customers. This advancement has outpaced N2 development at its current stage. Developers are planning to utilize the A14 for both client and AI/HPC applications.
insight: AI/HPC and smartphone industries will benefit from A14 performance boosts.

title: Florida man arrested after allegedly stealing $220,000 in crypto using malware hidden in Steam Games 8,000 devices infected
summary: A 21-year-old Florida man was arrested for allegedly stealing $220,000 in crypto using malware hidden in Steam games, infecting 8,000 devices.
insight: Malware in popular games poses significant threat to cryptocurrency security.

title: GameStop CEO says Sony's decision to go disc-less is 'totally irrelevant' claims software, including physical discs, accounts for only 12% of the company's business
summary: GameStop CEO Ryan Cohen downplays Sony's disc-less decision, citing collectibles as growth driver.
insight: GameStop's diversification reduces reliance on physical game sales.

title: Lawmakers want US government to ban memory chips from China, even in allied supply chains citing 'unacceptable risk' to national, economic, and supply chain security
summary: US lawmakers demand a ban on Chinese memory chip imports, citing national security concerns.
insight: Unacceptable risks to US national security and supply chain stability exist.

title: AI data centers must produce as much power as they use, Australia PM says new national AI framework will also ensure water efficiency and protect intellectual property rights
summary: Australia's PM introduces national AI framework for data center sustainability.
insight: Australia's vast land and renewable energy attract AI hyperscalers.

title: Intel Nova Lake leak points to Core Ultra Series 400 branding, staggered release next year hotly anticipated flagship 52-core desktop CPU might not arrive until late 2027
summary: Intel's Nova Lake desktop processors may arrive with Core Ultra branding, with a staggered release planned for next year, but the flagship 52-core CPU might not be available until 2027.
insight: Intel's flagship CPU may be delayed until late 2027.

title: China's 2.8-trillion-parameter Kimi K3 beats Claude Fable 5 in Frontend Code Arena benchmark Moonshot AI delivers largest open-weight AI model ever, as China works around U.S. compute limits
summary: Moonshot AI releases Kimi K3, a 2.8 trillion parameter AI model. This is described as the world's first open 3T-class system, surpassing previous compute limits. The model is a significant achievement in AI development.
insight: Breaking U.S. compute limits with a massive AI model advancement

title: Score $1,100 off this OLED gaming laptop with RTX 5070 Ti, the ultimate Back to School powerhouse also features a 24-core Ultra 9 290HX, 32GB of RAM, and a 1TB SSD
summary: Get the HP Omen Max laptop with OLED, RTX 5070 Ti, and 32GB RAM at a discounted price.
insight: Cutting-edge gaming performance at a significantly reduced cost.

title: MSI MAG Z890 Tomahawk Wifi II motherboard review: Loses features from the original, but shaves a few dollars off the price
summary: The MSI MAG Z890 Tomahawk Wifi II motherboard offers a budget-friendly option with reduced connectivity features, still providing a reasonably equipped Z890 board at a sub-$200 price.
insight: Budget-conscious buyers prioritize affordability over full-featured motherboards.

title: ASML looks to increase prices of its Low-NA EUV tools beyond existing productivity-based model company wants to capture the value of all the advantages its tools offer, not just wafer throughput improvements
summary: ASML plans to increase prices beyond its current productivity-based model, capturing all tool advantages. The company's value-based approach is expected to be maintained. TSMC expresses concerns about the potential price hike.
insight: ASML seeks to monetize benefits beyond wafer throughput improvements

title: M5Stack Core2 gets open-source firmware to reproduce OpenAI s Codex Micro features
summary: IAMLIUBO has created open-source firmware for the M5Stack Core2 to mimic Codex Micro features, allowing developers to easily monitor and control agents. This can be done without the need for a custom macro pad. The solution is more affordable and accessible than the original Codex Micro.
insight: Reproducing Codex Micro features at a lower cost is now possible

title: Best of Sensors: Siargo wins with MEMS Mass Flow Sensor
summary: Siargo's MEMS mass flow sensor FS4000 won Best Instrumentation and Test Solution at the Best of Sensors 2026 award for its ability to capture dynamic thermal transfer data for heat distribution and time.
insight: Accurate thermal monitoring enables precise temperature control and efficiency.

title: iPhone owners hit 87% loyalty rate this year, Android-to-iPhone switching drops to 12%
summary: According to CIRP, iPhone owners have a 87% loyalty rate this year, with only 12% switching from Android.
insight: Apple's strong ecosystem keeps users loyal and satisfied with their iOS experience.

title: Spotify is deleting millions of AI-generated music tracks to fend off spammers
summary: Spotify removed 75 million AI-generated tracks in 2025 to combat spam operations.
insight: Spam operations exploit AI-generated music for malicious marketing purposes.

title: Chinese memory maker CXMT's output has grown from 40,000 to 720,000 wafers in a few years, and it's closing in on Micron
summary: ChangXin Memory Technologies (CXMT) is experiencing rapid growth, increasing its output from 40,000 to 720,000 wafers in a few years. The Chinese memory and IDM manufacturer is expected to produce between 350,000 and 375,000 DRAM wafers per month by the end of 2026. This growth positions CXMT to close in on Micron's production levels.
insight: CXMT's rapid growth threatens Micron's market dominance in DRAM production.

title: OnePlus confirms exit from US and European markets, will replace OxygenOS with ColorOS
summary: OnePlus is exiting the US and European markets, replacing OxygenOS with ColorOS, and will honor warranty coverage for existing devices.
insight: Shift signals further integration with parent company Oppo

title: Japan's first national AI factory will use 27,500 GPUs across 382 Vera Rubin racks
summary: Japan's first national AI factory will utilize 27,500 GPUs and 13,750 CPUs for training trillion-parameter models.
insight: Massive AI computing infrastructure supports large-scale model training advancements.

title: Apple's OLED iPad mini could arrive this October, kicking off a major iPad overhaul
summary: Apple plans to release an OLED iPad mini in October, marking a major overhaul of its iPad lineup. A revised standard iPad is expected in 2027, followed by OLED-equipped iPad Air and Pro models. This move signals a significant shift towards OLED displays across the iPad series.
insight: Apple's transition to OLED displays across iPad lineup is imminent

title: The EU is forcing Google to give rival AI assistants the same access as Gemini on Android
summary: The EU is forcing Google to give rival AI assistants equal access on Android, affecting 60% of EU smartphones. This integration aims to turn chatbots into everyday assistants. The move could reshape the AI market.
insight: EU regulation could level the AI assistant playing field drastically.

title: This Lenovo laptop is the first to use inkjet-printed OLED, a technology that could make OLED screens cheaper
summary: Lenovo's R9000P laptop features an inkjet-printed OLED screen in China, starting at $1,300.
insight: Cheaper OLED screens could become a reality with this technology advancement.

title: Over 4,500 Google workers demand layoff protections as Alphabet's value hits $4.3 trillion
summary: Google's parent company Alphabet saw significant revenue and operating income growth, reaching a $4.3 trillion valuation. This success has been driven by the AI boom, like many other tech companies. Employees are now pushing for layoff protections.
insight: Rapid company growth raises concerns about employee job security.

title: A Doom developer says Microsoft "destroyed an immense amount of value" at id Software
summary: A former id Software developer criticizes Microsoft for announcing job cuts before new Doom DLC. The layoffs affected 20% of the Xbox staff. This came as a shock to employees.
insight: Poor timing of job cuts undermines employee trust and morale.

title: GTA 6 hacker who was sentenced to life in a secure hospital is moved to prison ahead of retrial
summary: A GTA 6 hacker, responsible for a massive leak in 2022, is being transferred to prison before a retrial. The hacker, previously 18 years old, claimed responsibility for an Uber breach. Details of their current status and retrial are not fully disclosed.
insight: High-profile hacking incidents can have severe real-world consequences for perpetrators.

title: Researchers synchronize 105,000 tiny magnetic oscillators in 45 nanoseconds, pointing to faster, low-power computing
summary: Researchers have successfully synchronized 105,000 tiny magnetic oscillators in 45 nanoseconds without an external clock. The oscillators, measuring 10-20 nanometers in width, naturally fall into sync through their magnetic spin. This achievement can lead to faster and more energy-efficient computing.
insight: Unlocking natural synchrony for ultra-fast, low-power computing breakthroughs
